# Security Checklist: Codex marketplace installation path

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-004 requirement quality for Codex install trust boundaries, sandbox and approval expectations, plugin payload safety, custom-agent registration, hooks, network access, and DOC-008 deferral.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist security` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Trust Boundary Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are repository marketplace, authoring source, generated payload, installed plugin cache, and custom-agent destination requirements defined as separate trust surfaces? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, FR-005; Data Model §Codex Surface]
- [x] CHK002 Are personal/local install requirements explicit that `dist/codex/speckit-pro/` is the installable payload and `speckit-pro/` is not an install target? [Completeness, Spec FR-004]
- [x] CHK003 Are bundled skills, skill metadata sidecars, and TOML custom agents specified as different Codex surfaces with different registration behavior? [Completeness, Spec FR-010; Research Decision 5]
- [x] CHK004 Does the install safety posture define sandbox mode, approval policy, network access, local cache trust, and external authentication expectations before install? [Completeness, Spec FR-013; Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK005 Does the bounded install-safety block require docs to identify bundled lifecycle hooks as plugin payload components and defer hook trust/policy depth to DOC-008 without implying silent hook execution or approval bypass? [Resolved: Spec FR-013; Data Model §Codex Surface and §Safety Notice; Contract §Required Route Sections; Quickstart §Manual Content Review]

## Approval And Network Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Are outside-workspace writes for default `~/.codex/agents/` distinguished from project-scoped `.codex/agents/` destination overrides? [Clarity, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK007 Are `$install` custom-agent registration writes described as local filesystem work from the installed plugin bundle, separate from Git-backed marketplace network access? [Clarity, Spec Session 2; Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK008 Are requirements explicit that users should approve only expected local writes of named SpecKit Pro TOML files or reject and rerun with narrower permissions? [Clarity, Spec Session 2; Data Model §Safety Notice]
- [x] CHK009 Do requirements avoid promising silent autonomous access, sandbox bypass, approval bypass, or unrestricted network access? [Consistency, Spec Session 3; Contract §Forbidden Content]
- [x] CHK010 Are network-disabled and Git-backed marketplace scenarios represented as first-install edge cases without expanding into full DOC-008 troubleshooting? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases; Research Decision 6]

## Source Evidence And Deferral

- [x] CHK011 Are official OpenAI Codex docs required as source of truth for plugin, marketplace, skills, subagents, permissions, approvals, sandbox, and network wording? [Traceability, Spec §Source Review Baseline; Research §Source Index]
- [x] CHK012 Are local source files required as evidence for Racecraft-specific paths, generated payload manifests, install skill behavior, custom-agent files, and Codex hook config? [Traceability, Plan §Source Evidence Read During Planning; Quickstart §Command-Snippet Source Review]
- [x] CHK013 Are security-reference topics intentionally deferred to DOC-008, including managed policy, hook/MCP/agent risk detail, stale-cache diagnosis, permission troubleshooting, update/remove/rollback, and full trust model? [Boundary, Spec FR-014; Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK014 Are README alignment requirements scoped so concise entry points cannot contradict the detailed docs-site safety guidance? [Consistency, Spec FR-001; Contract §Required README Alignment]

## Verification Readiness

- [x] CHK015 Is the manual command-snippet review required to cover every Codex command and path snippet changed by DOC-004 against official docs, CLI help, or checked-in source? [Measurability, Spec FR-017; Contract §Command And Path Snippet Review Table]
- [x] CHK016 Is PR readiness defined with docs-site validation, link validation, full SpecKit Pro shell tests, and manual source-backed command review? [Measurability, Spec FR-017; Plan §Validation Plan]
- [x] CHK017 Are forbidden implementation changes explicit for manifests, generated payloads, installer behavior, TOML templates, hooks, marketplace behavior, release automation, and runtime code? [Consistency, Spec FR-016; Plan §Declared File Operations]

## Notes

- Gap remediation: CHK005 was resolved by adding bounded lifecycle hook payload awareness to the DOC-004 spec, plan, research, data model, content contract, and quickstart while preserving DOC-008 ownership for hook trust analysis, policy tuning, and lifecycle security.

## Verification Pass

- [x] CHK018 Re-run confirms lifecycle hook configuration is listed as a Codex surface and plugin payload component. [Spec FR-013; Data Model §Codex Surface]
- [x] CHK019 Re-run confirms bundled hooks are described as subject to Codex sandbox, approval, and configured policy controls, without promising bypass or silent autonomous access. [Spec FR-013; Data Model §Safety Notice; Contract §Forbidden Content]
- [x] CHK020 Re-run confirms hook trust analysis, policy tuning, lifecycle security, and troubleshooting remain deferred to DOC-008. [Spec FR-014; Contract §Acceptance Checks]
- [x] CHK021 Re-run confirms source evidence covers local hook configuration through the manifest `hooks` field and `speckit-pro/codex-hooks.json`. [Quickstart §Command-Snippet Source Review; Plan §Source Evidence Read During Planning]
- [x] CHK022 Re-run confirms the hook remediation stayed documentation/planning-only and did not require manifest, generated payload, installer, TOML, hook, release, marketplace, or runtime changes. [Spec FR-016; Plan §Declared File Operations]
